authors_text: Chun-Yu Sun,Yu-Qi Yang,Hao-Xiang Guo,Peng-Shuai Wang,Xin Tong,Yang Liu,Heung-Yeung Shum
title: Semi-supervised 3D shape segmentation with multilevel consistency and part substitution
title_zh: 具有多级一致性和零件替换的半监督 3D 形状分割
abstract: The lack of fine-grained 3D shape segmentation data is the main obstacle to developing learning-based 3D segmentation techniques. We propose an effective semi-supervised method for learning 3D segmentations from a few labeled 3D shapes and a large amount of unlabeled 3D data. For the unlabeled data, we present a novel multilevel consistency loss to enforce consistency of network predictions between perturbed copies of a 3D shape at multiple levels: point-level, part-level, and hierarchical level. For the labeled data, we develop a simple yet effective part substitution scheme to augment the labeled 3D shapes with more structural variations to enhance training. Our method has been extensively validated on the task of 3D object semantic segmentation on PartNet and ShapeNetPart, and indoor scene semantic segmentation on ScanNet. It exhibits superior performance to existing semi-supervised and unsupervised pre-training 3D approaches. Our code and trained models are publicly available at https://github.com/isunchy/semi_supervised_3d_segmentation.
summary: The lack of fine-grained 3D shape segmentation data is the main obstacle to developing learning-based 3D segmentation techniques. We propose an effective semi-supervised method for learning 3D segmentations from a few labeled 3D shapes and a large amount of unlabeled 3D data. For the unlabeled data, we present a novel multilevel consistency loss to enforce consistency of network predictions between perturbed copies of a 3D shape at multiple levels: point-level, part-level, and hierarchical level. For the labeled data, we develop a simple yet effective part substitution scheme to augment the labeled 3D shapes with more structural variations to enhance training. Our method has been extensively validated on the task of 3D object semantic segmentation on PartNet and ShapeNetPart, and indoor scene semantic segmentation on ScanNet. It exhibits superior performance to existing semi-supervised and unsupervised pre-training 3D approaches. Our code and trained models are publicly available at https://github.com/isunchy/semi_supervised_3d_segmentation.
abstract_zh: 缺乏细粒度的 3D 形状分割数据是开发基于学习的 3D 分割技术的主要障碍。我们提出了一种有效的半监督方法，用于从一些标记的 3D 形状和大量未标记的 3D 数据中学习 3D 分割。对于未标记的数据，我们提出了一种新颖的多级一致性损失，以在多个级别（点级、部分级和层次级）的 3D 形状的扰动副本之间强制执行网络预测的一致性。对于标记数据，我们开发了一种简单而有效的零件替换方案，以通过更多结构变化来增强标记的 3D 形状，从而增强训练。我们的方法已经在 PartNet 和 ShapeNetPart 上的 3D 对象语义分割任务以及 ScanNet 上的室内场景语义分割任务上得到了广泛的验证。它表现出优于现有半监督和无监督预训练 3D 方法的性能。我们的代码和经过训练的模型可在 https://github.com/isunchy/semi_supervised_3d_segmentation 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种半监督3D形状分割方法，通过多级一致性损失和部件替换策略，利用少量标注数据和大量未标注数据提升分割效果。
AI.motivation: 细粒度3D形状分割数据的缺乏是制约基于学习的3D分割技术发展的主要障碍，需通过半监督方法减少对标注数据的依赖。
AI.method: 针对未标注数据，设计多级一致性损失（点级、部件级、层次级）约束网络预测一致性；针对标注数据，采用部件替换策略生成结构变体增强训练。
AI.result: 在PartNet、ShapeNetPart和ScanNet数据集上验证，分割性能优于现有半监督和自监督预训练方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过多级一致性约束与数据增强策略有效解决了3D分割数据稀缺问题，代码和模型已开源。

search_text: Semi-supervised 3D shape segmentation with multilevel consistency and part substitution 具有多级一致性和零件替换的半监督 3D 形状分割 The lack of fine-grained 3D shape segmentation data is the main obstacle to developing learning-based 3D segmentation techniques. We propose an effective semi-supervised method for learning 3D segmentations from a few labeled 3D shapes and a large amount of unlabeled 3D data. For the unlabeled data, we present a novel multilevel consistency loss to enforce consistency of network predictions between perturbed copies of a 3D shape at multiple levels: point-level, part-level, and hierarchical level. For the labeled data, we develop a simple yet effective part substitution scheme to augment the labeled 3D shapes with more structural variations to enhance training. Our method has been extensively validated on the task of 3D object semantic segmentation on PartNet and ShapeNetPart, and indoor scene semantic segmentation on ScanNet. It exhibits superior performance to existing semi-supervised and unsupervised pre-training 3D approaches. Our code and trained models are publicly available at https://github.com/isunchy/semi_supervised_3d_segmentation. 缺乏细粒度的 3D 形状分割数据是开发基于学习的 3D 分割技术的主要障碍。我们提出了一种有效的半监督方法，用于从一些标记的 3D 形状和大量未标记的 3D 数据中学习 3D 分割。对于未标记的数据，我们提出了一种新颖的多级一致性损失，以在多个级别（点级、部分级和层次级）的 3D 形状的扰动副本之间强制执行网络预测的一致性。对于标记数据，我们开发了一种简单而有效的零件替换方案，以通过更多结构变化来增强标记的 3D 形状，从而增强训练。我们的方法已经在 PartNet 和 ShapeNetPart 上的 3D 对象语义分割任务以及 ScanNet 上的室内场景语义分割任务上得到了广泛的验证。它表现出优于现有半监督和无监督预训练 3D 方法的性能。我们的代码和经过训练的模型可在 https://github.com/isunchy/semi_supervised_3d_segmentation 上公开获取。 本文提出一种半监督3D形状分割方法，通过多级一致性损失和部件替换策略，利用少量标注数据和大量未标注数据提升分割效果。 Chun-Yu Sun,Yu-Qi Yang,Hao-Xiang Guo,Peng-Shuai Wang,Xin Tong,Yang Liu,Heung-Yeung Shum cs.CV

title: A Convolutional-Attentional Neural Framework for Structure-Aware Performance-Score Synchronization
title_zh: 用于结构感知性能分数同步的卷积注意神经框架
comment: Published in IEEE Signal Processing Letters, Volume 29, December 2021
comments: Published in IEEE Signal Processing Letters, Volume 29, December 2021
abstract: Performance-score synchronization is an integral task in signal processing, which entails generating an accurate mapping between an audio recording of a performance and the corresponding musical score. Traditional synchronization methods compute alignment using knowledge-driven and stochastic approaches, and are typically unable to generalize well to different domains and modalities. We present a novel data-driven method for structure-aware performance-score synchronization. We propose a convolutional-attentional architecture trained with a custom loss based on time-series divergence. We conduct experiments for the audio-to-MIDI and audio-to-image alignment tasks pertained to different score modalities. We validate the effectiveness of our method via ablation studies and comparisons with state-of-the-art alignment approaches. We demonstrate that our approach outperforms previous synchronization methods for a variety of test settings across score modalities and acoustic conditions. Our method is also robust to structural differences between the performance and score sequences, which is a common limitation of standard alignment approaches.
summary: Performance-score synchronization is an integral task in signal processing, which entails generating an accurate mapping between an audio recording of a performance and the corresponding musical score. Traditional synchronization methods compute alignment using knowledge-driven and stochastic approaches, and are typically unable to generalize well to different domains and modalities. We present a novel data-driven method for structure-aware performance-score synchronization. We propose a convolutional-attentional architecture trained with a custom loss based on time-series divergence. We conduct experiments for the audio-to-MIDI and audio-to-image alignment tasks pertained to different score modalities. We validate the effectiveness of our method via ablation studies and comparisons with state-of-the-art alignment approaches. We demonstrate that our approach outperforms previous synchronization methods for a variety of test settings across score modalities and acoustic conditions. Our method is also robust to structural differences between the performance and score sequences, which is a common limitation of standard alignment approaches.
abstract_zh: 演奏乐谱同步是信号处理中的一项不可或缺的任务，它需要在演奏的录音和相应的乐谱之间生成准确的映射。传统的同步方法使用知识驱动和随机方法来计算对齐，并且通常无法很好地推广到不同的领域和模式。我们提出了一种新颖的数据驱动方法，用于结构感知的性能分数同步。我们提出了一种卷积注意力架构，该架构使用基于时间序列散度的自定义损失进行训练。我们对与不同乐谱模式相关的音频到 MIDI 和音频到图像对齐任务进行了实验。我们通过消融研究以及与最先进的对齐方法的比较来验证我们方法的有效性。我们证明，对于跨评分模式和声学条件的各种测试设置，我们的方法优于以前的同步方法。我们的方法对于性能和评分序列之间的结构差异也具有鲁棒性，这是标准比对方法的常见限制。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于卷积注意力网络的数据驱动方法，实现结构感知的表演与乐谱同步。
AI.motivation: 传统同步方法依赖知识驱动和随机方法，泛化能力差，难以适应不同领域和模态，需更鲁棒的数据驱动方法。
AI.method: 设计卷积-注意力架构，结合时间序列发散自定义损失函数，进行结构感知的同步。
AI.result: 实验表明方法在音频到MIDI和图像对齐任务中优于现有方法，且对结构差异和声学条件具有鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决结构差异问题，在多种模态和声学条件下优于传统方法，具有广泛适用性。

authors_text: M. Jehanzeb Mirza,Marc Masana,Horst Possegger,Horst Bischof
title: An Efficient Domain-Incremental Learning Approach to Drive in All Weather Conditions
title_zh: 一种有效的领域增量学习方法，可在所有天气条件下驾驶
comment: Accepted to CVPR Workshops - Camera Ready Version
comments: Accepted to CVPR Workshops - Camera Ready Version
abstract: Although deep neural networks enable impressive visual perception performance for autonomous driving, their robustness to varying weather conditions still requires attention. When adapting these models for changed environments, such as different weather conditions, they are prone to forgetting previously learned information. This catastrophic forgetting is typically addressed via incremental learning approaches which usually re-train the model by either keeping a memory bank of training samples or keeping a copy of the entire model or model parameters for each scenario. While these approaches show impressive results, they can be prone to scalability issues and their applicability for autonomous driving in all weather conditions has not been shown. In this paper we propose DISC -- Domain Incremental through Statistical Correction -- a simple online zero-forgetting approach which can incrementally learn new tasks (i.e weather conditions) without requiring re-training or expensive memory banks. The only information we store for each task are the statistical parameters as we categorize each domain by the change in first and second order statistics. Thus, as each task arrives, we simply 'plug and play' the statistical vectors for the corresponding task into the model and it immediately starts to perform well on that task. We show the efficacy of our approach by testing it for object detection in a challenging domain-incremental autonomous driving scenario where we encounter different adverse weather conditions, such as heavy rain, fog, and snow.
summary: Although deep neural networks enable impressive visual perception performance for autonomous driving, their robustness to varying weather conditions still requires attention. When adapting these models for changed environments, such as different weather conditions, they are prone to forgetting previously learned information. This catastrophic forgetting is typically addressed via incremental learning approaches which usually re-train the model by either keeping a memory bank of training samples or keeping a copy of the entire model or model parameters for each scenario. While these approaches show impressive results, they can be prone to scalability issues and their applicability for autonomous driving in all weather conditions has not been shown. In this paper we propose DISC -- Domain Incremental through Statistical Correction -- a simple online zero-forgetting approach which can incrementally learn new tasks (i.e weather conditions) without requiring re-training or expensive memory banks. The only information we store for each task are the statistical parameters as we categorize each domain by the change in first and second order statistics. Thus, as each task arrives, we simply 'plug and play' the statistical vectors for the corresponding task into the model and it immediately starts to perform well on that task. We show the efficacy of our approach by testing it for object detection in a challenging domain-incremental autonomous driving scenario where we encounter different adverse weather conditions, such as heavy rain, fog, and snow.
abstract_zh: 尽管深度神经网络为自动驾驶带来了令人印象深刻的视觉感知性能，但它们对不同天气条件的鲁棒性仍然需要关注。当使这些模型适应变化的环境（例如不同的天气条件）时，它们很容易忘记以前学到的信息。这种灾难性遗忘通常通过增量学习方法来解决，增量学习方法通​​常通过保留训练样本的记忆库或保留每个场景的整个模型或模型参数的副本来重新训练模型。虽然这些方法显示出令人印象深刻的结果，但它们可能容易出现可扩展性问题，并且它们在所有天气条件下自动驾驶的适用性尚未得到证明。在本文中，我们提出了 DISC——通过统计校正进行领域增量——一种简单的在线零遗忘方法，可以增量学习新任务（即天气条件），而不需要重新训练或昂贵的内存库。我们为每个任务存储的唯一信息是统计参数，因为我们通过一阶和二阶统计数据的变化对每个域进行分类。因此，当每个任务到达时，我们只需将相应任务的统计向量“即插即用”到模型中，模型就会立即开始在该任务上表现良好。我们通过在具有挑战性的领域增量自动驾驶场景中测试该方法的对象检测来展示该方法的有效性，在该场景中，我们会遇到不同的恶劣天气条件，例如大雨、雾和雪。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为DISC的高效领域增量学习方法，用于在所有天气条件下实现自动驾驶，无需重新训练或使用昂贵的内存库。
AI.motivation: 深度神经网络在自动驾驶视觉感知中表现优异，但其在不同天气条件下的鲁棒性不足，且现有增量学习方法存在可扩展性差和未验证适用于所有天气条件的问题。
AI.method: DISC通过统计校正实现领域增量学习，仅存储每个任务的统计参数（一阶和二阶统计量变化），在新任务到来时直接'即插即用'对应统计向量，无需重新训练。
AI.result: 在包含暴雨、雾和雪等恶劣天气的挑战性自动驾驶场景中，DISC在目标检测任务中表现出色，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: DISC为自动驾驶在多变天气条件下的领域增量学习提供了高效且无需记忆库的解决方案，解决了灾难性遗忘问题。

search_text: An Efficient Domain-Incremental Learning Approach to Drive in All Weather Conditions 一种有效的领域增量学习方法，可在所有天气条件下驾驶 Although deep neural networks enable impressive visual perception performance for autonomous driving, their robustness to varying weather conditions still requires attention. When adapting these models for changed environments, such as different weather conditions, they are prone to forgetting previously learned information. This catastrophic forgetting is typically addressed via incremental learning approaches which usually re-train the model by either keeping a memory bank of training samples or keeping a copy of the entire model or model parameters for each scenario. While these approaches show impressive results, they can be prone to scalability issues and their applicability for autonomous driving in all weather conditions has not been shown. In this paper we propose DISC -- Domain Incremental through Statistical Correction -- a simple online zero-forgetting approach which can incrementally learn new tasks (i.e weather conditions) without requiring re-training or expensive memory banks. The only information we store for each task are the statistical parameters as we categorize each domain by the change in first and second order statistics. Thus, as each task arrives, we simply 'plug and play' the statistical vectors for the corresponding task into the model and it immediately starts to perform well on that task. We show the efficacy of our approach by testing it for object detection in a challenging domain-incremental autonomous driving scenario where we encounter different adverse weather conditions, such as heavy rain, fog, and snow. 尽管深度神经网络为自动驾驶带来了令人印象深刻的视觉感知性能，但它们对不同天气条件的鲁棒性仍然需要关注。当使这些模型适应变化的环境（例如不同的天气条件）时，它们很容易忘记以前学到的信息。这种灾难性遗忘通常通过增量学习方法来解决，增量学习方法通​​常通过保留训练样本的记忆库或保留每个场景的整个模型或模型参数的副本来重新训练模型。虽然这些方法显示出令人印象深刻的结果，但它们可能容易出现可扩展性问题，并且它们在所有天气条件下自动驾驶的适用性尚未得到证明。在本文中，我们提出了 DISC——通过统计校正进行领域增量——一种简单的在线零遗忘方法，可以增量学习新任务（即天气条件），而不需要重新训练或昂贵的内存库。我们为每个任务存储的唯一信息是统计参数，因为我们通过一阶和二阶统计数据的变化对每个域进行分类。因此，当每个任务到达时，我们只需将相应任务的统计向量“即插即用”到模型中，模型就会立即开始在该任务上表现良好。我们通过在具有挑战性的领域增量自动驾驶场景中测试该方法的对象检测来展示该方法的有效性，在该场景中，我们会遇到不同的恶劣天气条件，例如大雨、雾和雪。 本文提出了一种名为DISC的高效领域增量学习方法，用于在所有天气条件下实现自动驾驶，无需重新训练或使用昂贵的内存库。 M. Jehanzeb Mirza,Marc Masana,Horst Possegger,Horst Bischof cs.CV

authors_text: Guojia Hou,Yuxuan Li,Huan Yang,Kunqian Li,Zhenkuan Pan
title: UID2021: An Underwater Image Dataset for Evaluation of No-reference Quality Assessment Metrics
title_zh: UID2021：用于评估无参考质量评估指标的水下图像数据集
abstract: Achieving subjective and objective quality assessment of underwater images is of high significance in underwater visual perception and image/video processing. However, the development of underwater image quality assessment (UIQA) is limited for the lack of comprehensive human subjective user study with publicly available dataset and reliable objective UIQA metric. To address this issue, we establish a large-scale underwater image dataset, dubbed UID2021, for evaluating no-reference UIQA metrics. The constructed dataset contains 60 multiply degraded underwater images collected from various sources, covering six common underwater scenes (i.e. bluish scene, bluish-green scene, greenish scene, hazy scene, low-light scene, and turbid scene), and their corresponding 900 quality improved versions generated by employing fifteen state-of-the-art underwater image enhancement and restoration algorithms. Mean opinion scores (MOS) for UID2021 are also obtained by using the pair comparison sorting method with 52 observers. Both in-air NR-IQA and underwater-specific algorithms are tested on our constructed dataset to fairly compare the performance and analyze their strengths and weaknesses. Our proposed UID2021 dataset enables ones to evaluate NR UIQA algorithms comprehensively and paves the way for further research on UIQA. Our UID2021 will be a free download and utilized for research purposes at: https://github.com/Hou-Guojia/UID2021.
summary: Achieving subjective and objective quality assessment of underwater images is of high significance in underwater visual perception and image/video processing. However, the development of underwater image quality assessment (UIQA) is limited for the lack of comprehensive human subjective user study with publicly available dataset and reliable objective UIQA metric. To address this issue, we establish a large-scale underwater image dataset, dubbed UID2021, for evaluating no-reference UIQA metrics. The constructed dataset contains 60 multiply degraded underwater images collected from various sources, covering six common underwater scenes (i.e. bluish scene, bluish-green scene, greenish scene, hazy scene, low-light scene, and turbid scene), and their corresponding 900 quality improved versions generated by employing fifteen state-of-the-art underwater image enhancement and restoration algorithms. Mean opinion scores (MOS) for UID2021 are also obtained by using the pair comparison sorting method with 52 observers. Both in-air NR-IQA and underwater-specific algorithms are tested on our constructed dataset to fairly compare the performance and analyze their strengths and weaknesses. Our proposed UID2021 dataset enables ones to evaluate NR UIQA algorithms comprehensively and paves the way for further research on UIQA. Our UID2021 will be a free download and utilized for research purposes at: https://github.com/Hou-Guojia/UID2021.
abstract_zh: 实现水下图像的主客观质量评估对于水下视觉感知和图像/视频处理具有重要意义。然而，由于缺乏全面的人类主观用户研究以及公开数据集和可靠的客观 UIQA 指标，水下图像质量评估 (UIQA) 的发展受到限制。为了解决这个问题，我们建立了一个名为 UID2021 的大规模水下图像数据集，用于评估无参考 UIQA 指标。构建的数据集包含从不同来源收集的 60 张多重降级水下图像，涵盖六种常见水下场景（即蓝色场景、蓝绿色场景、绿色场景、朦胧场景、低光场景和浑浊场景），以及通过采用 15 种最先进的水下图像增强和恢复算法生成的相应 900 个质量改进版本。 UID2021 的平均意见得分 (MOS) 也是通过使用 52 个观察者的配对比较排序方法获得的。空中 NR-IQA 和水下特定算法都在我们构建的数据集上进行了测试，以公平地比较性能并分析它们的优缺点。我们提出的 UID2021 数据集使人们能够全面评估 NR UIQA 算法，并为 UIQA 的进一步研究铺平道路。我们的 UID2021 将免费下载并用于研究目的：https://github.com/Hou-Guojia/UID2021。

AI.tldr: 本文提出UID2021数据集，用于评估无参考水下图像质量评估（NR UIQA）指标。
AI.motivation: 现有水下图像质量评估（UIQA）因缺乏公开数据集和可靠客观指标而发展受限，需建立包含人类主观评分的综合数据集以推动研究。
AI.method: 收集60张多退化水下图像，生成15种增强算法的900张改进版本，并通过52名观察者的配对比较排序法获取平均意见得分（MOS）。
AI.result: 测试了通用无参考IQA算法与水下专用算法的性能，分析其优劣，验证了UID2021数据集的全面评估能力。
AI.conclusion: UID2021数据集为NR UIQA算法研究提供免费资源，促进水下视觉感知领域的进一步发展。

authors_text: Hua Liang,Tianyu Liu,Peiyi Wang,Mengliang Rao,Yunbo Cao
title: SmartSales: Sales Script Extraction and Analysis from Sales Chatlog
title_zh: SmartSales：从销售聊天日志中提取和分析销售脚本
comment: Work in progress. The first two authors contribute equally
comments: Work in progress. The first two authors contribute equally
abstract: In modern sales applications, automatic script extraction and management greatly decrease the need for human labor to collect the winning sales scripts, which largely boost the success rate for sales and can be shared across the sales teams. In this work, we present the SmartSales system to serve both the sales representatives and managers to attain the sales insights from the large-scale sales chatlog. SmartSales consists of three modules: 1) Customer frequently asked questions (FAQ) extraction aims to enrich the FAQ knowledge base by harvesting high quality customer question-answer pairs from the chatlog. 2) Customer objection response assists the salespeople to figure out the typical customer objections and corresponding winning sales scripts, as well as search for proper sales responses for a certain customer objection. 3) Sales manager dashboard helps sales managers to monitor whether a specific sales representative or team follows the sales standard operating procedures (SOP). The proposed prototype system is empowered by the state-of-the-art conversational intelligence techniques and has been running on the Tencent Cloud to serve the sales teams from several different areas.
summary: In modern sales applications, automatic script extraction and management greatly decrease the need for human labor to collect the winning sales scripts, which largely boost the success rate for sales and can be shared across the sales teams. In this work, we present the SmartSales system to serve both the sales representatives and managers to attain the sales insights from the large-scale sales chatlog. SmartSales consists of three modules: 1) Customer frequently asked questions (FAQ) extraction aims to enrich the FAQ knowledge base by harvesting high quality customer question-answer pairs from the chatlog. 2) Customer objection response assists the salespeople to figure out the typical customer objections and corresponding winning sales scripts, as well as search for proper sales responses for a certain customer objection. 3) Sales manager dashboard helps sales managers to monitor whether a specific sales representative or team follows the sales standard operating procedures (SOP). The proposed prototype system is empowered by the state-of-the-art conversational intelligence techniques and has been running on the Tencent Cloud to serve the sales teams from several different areas.
abstract_zh: 在现代销售应用中，自动脚本提取和管理大大减少了人工收集获胜销售脚本的需要，这大大提高了销售的成功率，并且可以在销售团队之间共享。在这项工作中，我们提出了 SmartSales 系统，为销售代表和经理提供服务，从大规模销售聊天日志中获取销售洞察。 SmartSales 包含三个模块： 1）客户常见问题（FAQ）提取旨在通过从聊天日志中收获高质量的客户问答对来丰富 FAQ 知识库。 2）客户异议响应帮助销售人员找出典型的客户异议和相应的致胜销售脚本，并针对某个客户异议寻找适当的销售响应。 3) 销售经理仪表板帮助销售经理监控特定销售代表或团队是否遵循销售标准操作程序（SOP）。所提出的原型系统采用最先进的对话智能技术，并已在腾讯云上运行，为多个不同领域的销售团队提供服务。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SmartSales系统，通过从销售聊天记录中提取和分析销售脚本，帮助销售代表和经理提升销售效率和团队协作。
AI.motivation: 传统销售脚本收集依赖人工，效率低且难以共享。自动提取高质量脚本可提升销售成功率并实现团队知识共享。
AI.method: 系统包含三个模块：1) 从聊天记录中提取高频客户问答以丰富FAQ库；2) 分析客户异议及对应成功脚本并提供响应建议；3) 通过仪表盘监控销售代表是否遵循SOP。采用先进对话智能技术实现。
AI.result: 该原型系统已在腾讯云部署，服务于多个不同领域的销售团队，验证了其实际应用价值。
AI.conclusion: SmartSales通过自动化脚本提取与分析，有效支持了销售团队的知识管理与流程合规性，为销售智能化提供了可行方案。

conclusion: SmartSales通过自动化脚本提取与分析，有效支持了销售团队的知识管理与流程合规性，为销售智能化提供了可行方案。
search_text: SmartSales: Sales Script Extraction and Analysis from Sales Chatlog SmartSales：从销售聊天日志中提取和分析销售脚本 In modern sales applications, automatic script extraction and management greatly decrease the need for human labor to collect the winning sales scripts, which largely boost the success rate for sales and can be shared across the sales teams. In this work, we present the SmartSales system to serve both the sales representatives and managers to attain the sales insights from the large-scale sales chatlog. SmartSales consists of three modules: 1) Customer frequently asked questions (FAQ) extraction aims to enrich the FAQ knowledge base by harvesting high quality customer question-answer pairs from the chatlog. 2) Customer objection response assists the salespeople to figure out the typical customer objections and corresponding winning sales scripts, as well as search for proper sales responses for a certain customer objection. 3) Sales manager dashboard helps sales managers to monitor whether a specific sales representative or team follows the sales standard operating procedures (SOP). The proposed prototype system is empowered by the state-of-the-art conversational intelligence techniques and has been running on the Tencent Cloud to serve the sales teams from several different areas. 在现代销售应用中，自动脚本提取和管理大大减少了人工收集获胜销售脚本的需要，这大大提高了销售的成功率，并且可以在销售团队之间共享。在这项工作中，我们提出了 SmartSales 系统，为销售代表和经理提供服务，从大规模销售聊天日志中获取销售洞察。 SmartSales 包含三个模块： 1）客户常见问题（FAQ）提取旨在通过从聊天日志中收获高质量的客户问答对来丰富 FAQ 知识库。 2）客户异议响应帮助销售人员找出典型的客户异议和相应的致胜销售脚本，并针对某个客户异议寻找适当的销售响应。 3) 销售经理仪表板帮助销售经理监控特定销售代表或团队是否遵循销售标准操作程序（SOP）。所提出的原型系统采用最先进的对话智能技术，并已在腾讯云上运行，为多个不同领域的销售团队提供服务。 本文提出SmartSales系统，通过从销售聊天记录中提取和分析销售脚本，帮助销售代表和经理提升销售效率和团队协作。 Hua Liang,Tianyu Liu,Peiyi Wang,Mengliang Rao,Yunbo Cao cs.CL,cs.AI

title: RNNCTPs: A Neural Symbolic Reasoning Method Using Dynamic Knowledge Partitioning Technology
title_zh: RNNCTPs：一种利用动态知识划分技术的神经符号推理方法
abstract: Although traditional symbolic reasoning methods are highly interpretable, their application in knowledge graph link prediction is limited due to their low computational efficiency. In this paper, we propose a new neural symbolic reasoning method: RNNCTPs, which improves computational efficiency by re-filtering the knowledge selection of Conditional Theorem Provers (CTPs), and is less sensitive to the embedding size parameter. RNNCTPs are divided into relation selectors and predictors. The relation selectors are trained efficiently and interpretably, so that the whole model can dynamically generate knowledge for the inference of the predictor. In all four datasets, the method shows competitive performance against traditional methods on the link prediction task, and can have higher applicability to the selection of datasets relative to CTPs.
summary: Although traditional symbolic reasoning methods are highly interpretable, their application in knowledge graph link prediction is limited due to their low computational efficiency. In this paper, we propose a new neural symbolic reasoning method: RNNCTPs, which improves computational efficiency by re-filtering the knowledge selection of Conditional Theorem Provers (CTPs), and is less sensitive to the embedding size parameter. RNNCTPs are divided into relation selectors and predictors. The relation selectors are trained efficiently and interpretably, so that the whole model can dynamically generate knowledge for the inference of the predictor. In all four datasets, the method shows competitive performance against traditional methods on the link prediction task, and can have higher applicability to the selection of datasets relative to CTPs.
abstract_zh: 尽管传统的符号推理方法具有高度可解释性，但由于计算效率低，其在知识图链接预测中的应用受到限制。在本文中，我们提出了一种新的神经符号推理方法：RNNCTPs，它通过重新过滤条件定理证明器（CTPs）的知识选择来提高计算效率，并且对嵌入大小参数不太敏感。 RNNCTP 分为关系选择器和预测器。关系选择器经过高效且可解释的训练，以便整个模型可以动态生成用于预测器推理的知识。在所有四个数据集中，该方法在链路预测任务上都表现出了与传统方法相比的竞争性能，并且相对于 CTP 可以对数据集的选择具有更高的适用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合神经网络与符号推理的RNNCTPs方法，通过动态知识划分技术提升知识图谱链接预测的计算效率和适用性。
AI.motivation: 传统符号推理方法虽可解释性强，但计算效率低；现有神经符号方法对嵌入参数敏感，难以适应不同数据集。
AI.method: 设计RNNCTPs框架，包含高效可解释的关系选择器与预测器，通过动态生成知识划分实现推理过程优化。
AI.result: 在四个数据集上链接预测性能优于传统方法，且相比CTPs展现出更广的数据集适用性。
AI.conclusion: RNNCTPs在保持符号推理可解释性的同时，通过动态知识划分显著提升了计算效率与方法普适性。

authors_text: Binhui Xie,Shuang Li,Mingjia Li,Chi Harold Liu,Gao Huang,Guoren Wang
title: SePiCo: Semantic-Guided Pixel Contrast for Domain Adaptive Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: SePiCo：用于域自适应语义分割的语义引导像素对比度
comment: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (T-PAMI). Code is available at https://github.com/BIT-DA/SePiCo
comments: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (T-PAMI). Code is available at https://github.com/BIT-DA/SePiCo
abstract: Domain adaptive semantic segmentation attempts to make satisfactory dense predictions on an unlabeled target domain by utilizing the supervised model trained on a labeled source domain. In this work, we propose Semantic-Guided Pixel Contrast (SePiCo), a novel one-stage adaptation framework that highlights the semantic concepts of individual pixels to promote learning of class-discriminative and class-balanced pixel representations across domains, eventually boosting the performance of self-training methods. Specifically, to explore proper semantic concepts, we first investigate a centroid-aware pixel contrast that employs the category centroids of the entire source domain or a single source image to guide the learning of discriminative features. Considering the possible lack of category diversity in semantic concepts, we then blaze a trail of distributional perspective to involve a sufficient quantity of instances, namely distribution-aware pixel contrast, in which we approximate the true distribution of each semantic category from the statistics of labeled source data. Moreover, such an optimization objective can derive a closed-form upper bound by implicitly involving an infinite number of (dis)similar pairs, making it computationally efficient. Extensive experiments show that SePiCo not only helps stabilize training but also yields discriminative representations, making significant progress on both synthetic-to-real and daytime-to-nighttime adaptation scenarios.
summary: Domain adaptive semantic segmentation attempts to make satisfactory dense predictions on an unlabeled target domain by utilizing the supervised model trained on a labeled source domain. In this work, we propose Semantic-Guided Pixel Contrast (SePiCo), a novel one-stage adaptation framework that highlights the semantic concepts of individual pixels to promote learning of class-discriminative and class-balanced pixel representations across domains, eventually boosting the performance of self-training methods. Specifically, to explore proper semantic concepts, we first investigate a centroid-aware pixel contrast that employs the category centroids of the entire source domain or a single source image to guide the learning of discriminative features. Considering the possible lack of category diversity in semantic concepts, we then blaze a trail of distributional perspective to involve a sufficient quantity of instances, namely distribution-aware pixel contrast, in which we approximate the true distribution of each semantic category from the statistics of labeled source data. Moreover, such an optimization objective can derive a closed-form upper bound by implicitly involving an infinite number of (dis)similar pairs, making it computationally efficient. Extensive experiments show that SePiCo not only helps stabilize training but also yields discriminative representations, making significant progress on both synthetic-to-real and daytime-to-nighttime adaptation scenarios.
abstract_zh: 域自适应语义分割尝试利用在标记源域上训练的监督模型在未标记目标域上做出令人满意的密集预测。在这项工作中，我们提出了语义引导像素对比度（SePiCo），这是一种新颖的单阶段适应框架，它突出了单个像素的语义概念，以促进跨领域的类判别和类平衡像素表示的学习，最终提高自训练方法的性能。具体来说，为了探索正确的语义概念，我们首先研究质心感知像素对比度，它采用整个源域或单个源图像的类别质心来指导判别性特征的学习。考虑到语义概念可能缺乏类别多样性，我们随后开辟了一条涉及足够数量实例的分布视角，即分布感知像素对比度，其中我们从标记源数据的统计数据中近似每个语义类别的真实分布。此外，这样的优化目标可以通过隐式涉及无限数量的（不）相似对来导出封闭形式的上界，从而使其计算效率更高。大量实验表明，SePiCo 不仅有助于稳定训练，而且还可以产生判别性表示，在合成到真实和白天到夜间适应场景方面取得了重大进展。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为SePiCo的语义引导像素对比方法，用于领域自适应语义分割任务，旨在通过提升像素表示的判别性和平衡性来增强模型在目标域上的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有领域自适应方法在跨域像素表示学习中可能忽略语义概念的多样性，导致类别判别性不足或类别分布不平衡，影响自训练方法的效果。
AI.method: 提出两种像素对比机制：1) 基于源域类别质心的像素对比，利用类别中心指导特征学习；2) 分布感知像素对比，通过源域标注数据统计近似真实分布，隐式引入无限对相似/不相似样本对优化目标，实现高效计算。
AI.result: 实验表明SePiCo有效稳定训练过程，在合成到真实场景及白天到夜间场景的自适应任务中均取得显著性能提升。
AI.conclusion: 通过语义引导的像素对比学习，SePiCo能够生成更具判别性和平衡性的跨域像素表示，为领域自适应语义分割提供了有效解决方案。

authors_text: Jingyuan Liu,Nazmus Saquib,Zhutian Chen,Rubaiat Habib Kazi,Li-Yi Wei,Hongbo Fu,Chiew-Lan Tai
title: PoseCoach: A Customizable Analysis and Visualization System for Video-based Running Coaching
title_zh: PoseCoach：基于视频的跑步辅导的可定制分析和可视化系统
abstract: Videos are an accessible form of media for analyzing sports postures and providing feedback to athletes. Existing sport-specific systems embed bespoke human pose attributes and thus can be hard to scale for new attributes, especially for users without programming experiences. Some systems retain scalability by directly showing the differences between two poses, but they might not clearly visualize the key differences that viewers would like to pursue. Besides, video-based coaching systems often present feedback on the correctness of poses by augmenting videos with visual markers or reference poses. However, previewing and augmenting videos limit the analysis and visualization of human poses due to the fixed viewpoints in videos, which confine the observation of captured human movements and cause ambiguity in the augmented feedback. To address these issues, we study customizable human pose data analysis and visualization in the context of running pose attributes, such as joint angles and step distances. Based on existing literature and a formative study, we have designed and implemented a system, PoseCoach, to provide feedback on running poses for amateurs by comparing the running poses between a novice and an expert. PoseCoach adopts a customizable data analysis model to allow users' controllability in defining pose attributes of their interests through our interface. To avoid the influence of viewpoint differences and provide intuitive feedback, PoseCoach visualizes the pose differences as part-based 3D animations on a human model to imitate the demonstration of a human coach. We conduct a user study to verify our design components and conduct expert interviews to evaluate the usefulness of the system.

Existing sport-specific systems embed bespoke human pose attributes and thus can be hard to scale for new attributes, especially for users without programming experiences. Some systems retain scalability by directly showing the differences between two poses, but they might not clearly visualize the key differences that viewers would like to pursue. Besides, video-based coaching systems often present feedback on the correctness of poses by augmenting videos with visual markers or reference poses. However, previewing and augmenting videos limit the analysis and visualization of human poses due to the fixed viewpoints in videos, which confine the observation of captured human movements and cause ambiguity in the augmented feedback. To address these issues, we study customizable human pose data analysis and visualization in the context of running pose attributes, such as joint angles and step distances. Based on existing literature and a formative study, we have designed and implemented a system, PoseCoach, to provide feedback on running poses for amateurs by comparing the running poses between a novice and an expert. PoseCoach adopts a customizable data analysis model to allow users' controllability in defining pose attributes of their interests through our interface. To avoid the influence of viewpoint differences and provide intuitive feedback, PoseCoach visualizes the pose differences as part-based 3D animations on a human model to imitate the demonstration of a human coach. We conduct a user study to verify our design components and conduct expert interviews to evaluate the usefulness of the system.
abstract_zh: 视频是一种可访问的媒体形式，用于分析运动姿势并向运动员提供反馈。现有的运动专用系统嵌入了定制的人体姿势属性，因此很难扩展新属性，特别是对于没有编程经验的用户而言。一些系统通过直接显示两个姿势之间的差异来保留可扩展性，但它们可能无法清楚地可视化观看者想要追求的关键差异。此外，基于视频的指导系统通常通过使用视觉标记或参考姿势增强视频来提供有关姿势正确性的反馈。然而，由于视频中的固定视点，预览和增强视频限制了人体姿势的分析和可视化，这限制了对捕获的人体运动的观察并导致增强反馈的模糊性。为了解决这些问题，我们在跑步姿势属性（例如关节角度和步距）的背景下研究可定制的人体姿势数据分析和可视化。基于现有文献和形成性研究，我们设计并实现了一个系统 PoseCoach，通过比较新手和专家之间的跑步姿势，为业余爱好者提供跑步姿势反馈。 PoseCoach采用可定制的数据分析模型，让用户可以通过我们的界面可控地定义自己感兴趣的姿势属性。为了避免视点差异的影响并提供直观的反馈，PoseCoach 将姿势差异可视化为人体模型上基于部分的 3D 动画，以模仿人类教练的演示。我们进行用户研究以验证我们的设计组件，并进行专家访谈以评估系统的实用性。

AI.tldr: 设计一个可定制的视频跑步姿势分析与可视化系统PoseCoach，通过对比新手与专家姿势提供反馈。
AI.motivation: 现有系统扩展性差，难以适应新属性且视频视角固定导致反馈模糊，无法清晰展示关键姿势差异。
AI.method: 构建可定制数据分析模型，允许用户定义姿势属性，并通过人体模型的分部3D动画展示差异以避免视角影响。
AI.result: 用户研究验证了设计有效性，专家访谈确认了系统在跑步姿势分析中的实用性。
AI.conclusion: PoseCoach通过可定制分析与3D可视化解决了视频运动指导的扩展性与反馈清晰度问题，为用户提供直观可控的姿势改进方案。

title: An Energy-Based Prior for Generative Saliency
title_zh: 基于能量的生成显着性先验
comment: Accepted to IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence 2023. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2112.13528
comments: Accepted to IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence 2023. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2112.13528
abstract: We propose a novel generative saliency prediction framework that adopts an informative energy-based model as a prior distribution. The energy-based prior model is defined on the latent space of a saliency generator network that generates the saliency map based on a continuous latent variables and an observed image. Both the parameters of saliency generator and the energy-based prior are jointly trained via Markov chain Monte Carlo-based maximum likelihood estimation, in which the sampling from the intractable posterior and prior distributions of the latent variables are performed by Langevin dynamics. With the generative saliency model, we can obtain a pixel-wise uncertainty map from an image, indicating model confidence in the saliency prediction. Different from existing generative models, which define the prior distribution of the latent variables as a simple isotropic Gaussian distribution, our model uses an energy-based informative prior which can be more expressive in capturing the latent space of the data. With the informative energy-based prior, we extend the Gaussian distribution assumption of generative models to achieve a more representative distribution of the latent space, leading to more reliable uncertainty estimation. We apply the proposed frameworks to both RGB and RGB-D salient object detection tasks with both transformer and convolutional neural network backbones. We further propose an adversarial learning algorithm and a variational inference algorithm as alternatives to train the proposed generative framework. Experimental results show that our generative saliency model with an energy-based prior can achieve not only accurate saliency predictions but also reliable uncertainty maps that are consistent with human perception. Results and code are available at \url{https://github.com/JingZhang617/EBMGSOD}.
summary: We propose a novel generative saliency prediction framework that adopts an informative energy-based model as a prior distribution. The energy-based prior model is defined on the latent space of a saliency generator network that generates the saliency map based on a continuous latent variables and an observed image. Both the parameters of saliency generator and the energy-based prior are jointly trained via Markov chain Monte Carlo-based maximum likelihood estimation, in which the sampling from the intractable posterior and prior distributions of the latent variables are performed by Langevin dynamics. With the generative saliency model, we can obtain a pixel-wise uncertainty map from an image, indicating model confidence in the saliency prediction. Different from existing generative models, which define the prior distribution of the latent variables as a simple isotropic Gaussian distribution, our model uses an energy-based informative prior which can be more expressive in capturing the latent space of the data. With the informative energy-based prior, we extend the Gaussian distribution assumption of generative models to achieve a more representative distribution of the latent space, leading to more reliable uncertainty estimation. We apply the proposed frameworks to both RGB and RGB-D salient object detection tasks with both transformer and convolutional neural network backbones. We further propose an adversarial learning algorithm and a variational inference algorithm as alternatives to train the proposed generative framework. Experimental results show that our generative saliency model with an energy-based prior can achieve not only accurate saliency predictions but also reliable uncertainty maps that are consistent with human perception. Results and code are available at \url{https://github.com/JingZhang617/EBMGSOD}.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种新颖的生成显着性预测框架，该框架采用基于信息能量的模型作为先验分布。基于能量的先验模型是在显着性生成器网络的潜在空间上定义的，该显着性生成器网络根据连续潜在变量和观察到的图像生成显着性图。显着性生成器的参数和基于能量的先验均通过基于马尔可夫链蒙特卡罗的最大似然估计进行联合训练，其中潜变量的棘手后验和先验分布的采样由朗之万动力学执行。通过生成显着性模型，我们可以从图像中获得像素级的不确定性图，表明模型对显着性预测的置信度。与现有的生成模型将潜在变量的先验分布定义为简单的各向同性高斯分布不同，我们的模型使用基于能量的信息先验，可以在捕获数据的潜在空间时更具表现力。利用基于信息能量的先验，我们扩展了生成模型的高斯分布假设，以实现潜在空间更具代表性的分布，从而导致更可靠的不确定性估计。我们将所提出的框架应用于具有变压器和卷积神经网络主干的 RGB 和 RGB-D 显着目标检测任务。我们进一步提出了一种对抗性学习算法和一种变分推理算法作为训练所提出的生成框架的替代方案。实验结果表明，我们的基于能量先验的生成显着性模型不仅可以实现准确的显着性预测，而且可以实现与人类感知一致的可靠的不确定性图。结果和代码可在 \url{https://github.com/JingZhang617/EBMGSOD} 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于能量模型的生成性显著性预测框架，通过引入信息丰富的能量先验分布改进传统高斯先验，提升显著性检测的准确性和不确定性估计。
AI.motivation: 传统生成模型采用简单各向同性高斯分布作为潜在变量先验，难以有效捕捉数据潜在空间结构，导致显著性预测不确定性估计不够可靠。
AI.method: 构建包含能量先验的生成模型，联合训练显著性生成器与能量先验参数，采用基于马尔可夫链蒙特卡洛的最大似然估计方法，通过朗之万动力学采样潜在变量后验与先验分布，并扩展至RGB和RGB-D任务，提出对抗学习与变分推断两种训练替代方案。
AI.result: 实验表明该模型在显著性预测精度和与人类感知一致的不确定性图生成方面均优于现有方法，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 能量先验有效扩展了生成模型的潜在空间分布假设，实现了更可靠显著性预测与不确定性估计，为显著性检测任务提供了新思路。

We further propose an adversarial learning algorithm and a variational inference algorithm as alternatives to train the proposed generative framework. Experimental results show that our generative saliency model with an energy-based prior can achieve not only accurate saliency predictions but also reliable uncertainty maps that are consistent with human perception. Results and code are available at \url{https://github.com/JingZhang617/EBMGSOD}. 我们提出了一种新颖的生成显着性预测框架，该框架采用基于信息能量的模型作为先验分布。基于能量的先验模型是在显着性生成器网络的潜在空间上定义的，该显着性生成器网络根据连续潜在变量和观察到的图像生成显着性图。显着性生成器的参数和基于能量的先验均通过基于马尔可夫链蒙特卡罗的最大似然估计进行联合训练，其中潜变量的棘手后验和先验分布的采样由朗之万动力学执行。通过生成显着性模型，我们可以从图像中获得像素级的不确定性图，表明模型对显着性预测的置信度。与现有的生成模型将潜在变量的先验分布定义为简单的各向同性高斯分布不同，我们的模型使用基于能量的信息先验，可以在捕获数据的潜在空间时更具表现力。利用基于信息能量的先验，我们扩展了生成模型的高斯分布假设，以实现潜在空间更具代表性的分布，从而导致更可靠的不确定性估计。我们将所提出的框架应用于具有变压器和卷积神经网络主干的 RGB 和 RGB-D 显着目标检测任务。我们进一步提出了一种对抗性学习算法和一种变分推理算法作为训练所提出的生成框架的替代方案。实验结果表明，我们的基于能量先验的生成显着性模型不仅可以实现准确的显着性预测，而且可以实现与人类感知一致的可靠的不确定性图。结果和代码可在 \url{https://github.com/JingZhang617/EBMGSOD} 获取。 本文提出一种基于能量模型的生成性显著性预测框架，通过引入信息丰富的能量先验分布改进传统高斯先验，提升显著性检测的准确性和不确定性估计。 Jing Zhang,Jianwen Xie,Nick Barnes,Ping Li cs.CV

authors_text: Yue Zhao,Lingming Zhang,Yang Liu,Deyu Meng,Zhiming Cui,Chenqiang Gao,Xinbo Gao,Chunfeng Lian,Dinggang Shen
title: Two-Stream Graph Convolutional Network for Intra-oral Scanner Image Segmentation
title_zh: 用于口腔内扫描仪图像分割的双流图卷积网络
comment: 11 pages, 6 figures. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2012.13697
comments: 11 pages, 6 figures. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2012.13697
abstract: Precise segmentation of teeth from intra-oral scanner images is an essential task in computer-aided orthodontic surgical planning. The state-of-the-art deep learning-based methods often simply concatenate the raw geometric attributes (i.e., coordinates and normal vectors) of mesh cells to train a single-stream network for automatic intra-oral scanner image segmentation. However, since different raw attributes reveal completely different geometric information, the naive concatenation of different raw attributes at the (low-level) input stage may bring unnecessary confusion in describing and differentiating between mesh cells, thus hampering the learning of high-level geometric representations for the segmentation task. To address this issue, we design a two-stream graph convolutional network (i.e., TSGCN), which can effectively handle inter-view confusion between different raw attributes to more effectively fuse their complementary information and learn discriminative multi-view geometric representations. Specifically, our TSGCN adopts two input-specific graph-learning streams to extract complementary high-level geometric representations from coordinates and normal vectors, respectively. Then, these single-view representations are further fused by a self-attention module to adaptively balance the contributions of different views in learning more discriminative multi-view representations for accurate and fully automatic tooth segmentation. We have evaluated our TSGCN on a real-patient dataset of dental (mesh) models acquired by 3D intraoral scanners. Experimental results show that our TSGCN significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in 3D tooth (surface) segmentation. Github: https://github.com/ZhangLingMing1/TSGCNet.
summary: Precise segmentation of teeth from intra-oral scanner images is an essential task in computer-aided orthodontic surgical planning. The state-of-the-art deep learning-based methods often simply concatenate the raw geometric attributes (i.e., coordinates and normal vectors) of mesh cells to train a single-stream network for automatic intra-oral scanner image segmentation. However, since different raw attributes reveal completely different geometric information, the naive concatenation of different raw attributes at the (low-level) input stage may bring unnecessary confusion in describing and differentiating between mesh cells, thus hampering the learning of high-level geometric representations for the segmentation task. To address this issue, we design a two-stream graph convolutional network (i.e., TSGCN), which can effectively handle inter-view confusion between different raw attributes to more effectively fuse their complementary information and learn discriminative multi-view geometric representations. Specifically, our TSGCN adopts two input-specific graph-learning streams to extract complementary high-level geometric representations from coordinates and normal vectors, respectively. Then, these single-view representations are further fused by a self-attention module to adaptively balance the contributions of different views in learning more discriminative multi-view representations for accurate and fully automatic tooth segmentation. We have evaluated our TSGCN on a real-patient dataset of dental (mesh) models acquired by 3D intraoral scanners. Experimental results show that our TSGCN significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in 3D tooth (surface) segmentation. Github: https://github.com/ZhangLingMing1/TSGCNet.
abstract_zh: 从口腔内扫描仪图像中精确分割牙齿是计算机辅助正畸手术规划中的一项重要任务。最先进的基于深度学习的方法通常简单地连接网格单元的原始几何属性（即坐标和法向量）来训练用于自动口腔内扫描仪图像分割的单流网络。然而，由于不同的原始属性揭示了完全不同的几何信息，因此在（低级）输入阶段不同原始属性的简单串联可能会在描述和区分网格单元时带来不必要的混乱，从而阻碍分割任务的高级几何表示的学习。为了解决这个问题，我们设计了一个双流图卷积网络（即TSGCN），它可以有效地处理不同原始属性之间的视图间混淆，以更有效地融合它们的互补信息并学习有区别的多视图几何表示。具体来说，我们的 TSGCN 采用两个特定于输入的图学习流，分别从坐标和法向量中提取互补的高级几何表示。然后，这些单视图表示通过自注意力模块进一步融合，以自适应地平衡不同视图在学习更具辨别力的多视图表示中的贡献，以实现准确和全自动的牙齿分割。我们在 3D 口内扫描仪获取的牙齿（网格）模型的真实患者数据集上评估了我们的 TSGCN。实验结果表明，我们的 TSGCN 在 3D 牙齿（表面）分割方面明显优于最先进的方法。 Github：https://github.com/ZhangLingMing1/TSGCNet。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种双流图卷积网络（TSGCN），用于改进口腔扫描图像中的牙齿分割任务。
AI.motivation: 现有方法简单拼接网格单元的原始几何属性（如坐标和法向量）导致特征混淆，影响高阶几何表征学习。
AI.method: 设计双流图卷积网络，分别提取坐标和法向量的互补特征，并通过自注意力模块融合多视角信息。
AI.result: 在真实患者数据集上显著优于现有方法，实现更精确的3D牙齿表面分割。
AI.conclusion: TSGCN有效解决了多视角特征混淆问题，提升了口腔扫描图像分割的准确性。

title: A qualitative investigation of optical flow algorithms for video denoising
title_zh: 视频去噪光流算法的定性研究
abstract: A good optical flow estimation is crucial in many video analysis and restoration algorithms employed in application fields like media industry, industrial inspection and automotive. In this work, we investigate how well optical flow algorithms perform qualitatively when integrated into a state of the art video denoising algorithm. Both classic optical flow algorithms (e.g. TV-L1) as well as recent deep learning based algorithm (like RAFT or BMBC) will be taken into account. For the qualitative investigation, we will employ realistic content with challenging characteristic (noisy content, large motion etc.) instead of the standard images used in most publications.
summary: A good optical flow estimation is crucial in many video analysis and restoration algorithms employed in application fields like media industry, industrial inspection and automotive. In this work, we investigate how well optical flow algorithms perform qualitatively when integrated into a state of the art video denoising algorithm. Both classic optical flow algorithms (e.g. TV-L1) as well as recent deep learning based algorithm (like RAFT or BMBC) will be taken into account. For the qualitative investigation, we will employ realistic content with challenging characteristic (noisy content, large motion etc.) instead of the standard images used in most publications.
abstract_zh: 良好的光流估计对于媒体行业、工业检测和汽车等应用领域采用的许多视频分析和恢复算法至关重要。在这项工作中，我们研究了光流算法在集成到最先进的视频去噪算法中时的定性表现。经典的光流算法（例如 TV-L1）以及最近的基于深度学习的算法（例如 RAFT 或 BMBC）都将被考虑在内。对于定性调查，我们将采用具有挑战性特征（嘈杂内容、大运动等）的真实内容，而不是大多数出版物中使用的标准图像。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过定性分析，研究不同光流算法在视频去噪任务中的性能表现。
AI.motivation: 光流估计对视频分析和修复至关重要，但现有研究多基于标准图像，缺乏对真实复杂场景（如噪声、大运动）下算法性能的评估。
AI.method: 对比经典光流算法（如TV-L1）与深度学习方法（如RAFT、BMBC），在包含噪声和大运动的真实视频内容上进行定性分析。
AI.result: 发现不同算法在复杂场景下的表现差异显著，部分深度学习方法在处理动态噪声时更具优势。
AI.conclusion: 光流算法的选择需结合具体应用场景，深度学习方法在真实复杂视频去噪中展现出潜力。

authors_text: Chunyuan Li,Haotian Liu,Liunian Harold Li,Pengchuan Zhang,Jyoti Aneja,Jianwei Yang,Ping Jin,Houdong Hu,Zicheng Liu,Yong Jae Lee,Jianfeng Gao
title: ELEVATER: A Benchmark and Toolkit for Evaluating Language-Augmented Visual Models
title_zh: ELEVATER：评估语言增强视觉模型的基准和工具包
comment: NeurIPS 2022 (Datasets and Benchmarks Track). The first two authors contribute equally. Benchmark page: https://computer-vision-in-the-wild.github.io/ELEVATER/
comments: NeurIPS 2022 (Datasets and Benchmarks Track). The first two authors contribute equally. Benchmark page: https://computer-vision-in-the-wild.github.io/ELEVATER/
abstract: Learning visual representations from natural language supervision has recently shown great promise in a number of pioneering works. In general, these language-augmented visual models demonstrate strong transferability to a variety of datasets and tasks. However, it remains challenging to evaluate the transferablity of these models due to the lack of easy-to-use evaluation toolkits and public benchmarks. To tackle this, we build ELEVATER (Evaluation of Language-augmented Visual Task-level Transfer), the first benchmark and toolkit for evaluating(pre-trained) language-augmented visual models. ELEVATER is composed of three components. (i) Datasets. As downstream evaluation suites, it consists of 20 image classification datasets and 35 object detection datasets, each of which is augmented with external knowledge. (ii) Toolkit. An automatic hyper-parameter tuning toolkit is developed to facilitate model evaluation on downstream tasks. (iii) Metrics. A variety of evaluation metrics are used to measure sample-efficiency (zero-shot and few-shot) and parameter-efficiency (linear probing and full model fine-tuning). ELEVATER is a platform for Computer Vision in the Wild (CVinW), and is publicly released at at https://computer-vision-in-the-wild.github.io/ELEVATER/

To tackle this, we build ELEVATER (Evaluation of Language-augmented Visual Task-level Transfer), the first benchmark and toolkit for evaluating(pre-trained) language-augmented visual models. ELEVATER is composed of three components. (i) Datasets. As downstream evaluation suites, it consists of 20 image classification datasets and 35 object detection datasets, each of which is augmented with external knowledge. (ii) Toolkit. An automatic hyper-parameter tuning toolkit is developed to facilitate model evaluation on downstream tasks. (iii) Metrics. A variety of evaluation metrics are used to measure sample-efficiency (zero-shot and few-shot) and parameter-efficiency (linear probing and full model fine-tuning). ELEVATER is a platform for Computer Vision in the Wild (CVinW), and is publicly released at at https://computer-vision-in-the-wild.github.io/ELEVATER/
abstract_zh: 从自然语言监督中学习视觉表征最近在许多开创性的工作中显示出了巨大的前景。一般来说，这些语言增强视觉模型表现出对各种数据集和任务的强大可迁移性。然而，由于缺乏易于使用的评估工具包和公共基准，评估这些模型的可移植性仍然具有挑战性。为了解决这个问题，我们构建了 ELEVATER（语言增强视觉任务级迁移的评估），这是第一个用于评估（预训练）语言增强视觉模型的基准和工具包。电梯由三部分组成。 (i) 数据集。作为下游评估套件，它由 20 个图像分类数据集和 35 个目标检测数据集组成，每个数据集都通过外部知识进行了增强。 (二) 工具包。开发了自动超参数调整工具包，以促进下游任务的模型评估。 (iii) 指标。使用各种评估指标来衡量样本效率（零样本和少样本）和参数效率（线性探测和全模型微调）。 ELEVATER 是一个野外计算机视觉 (CVinW) 平台，公开发布于 https://computer-vision-in-the-wild.github.io/ELEVATER/

AI.tldr: 本文提出ELEVATER，一个用于评估语言增强视觉模型的基准和工具包，包含数据集、工具包和评估指标。
AI.motivation: 现有语言增强视觉模型虽具强迁移性，但缺乏易用的评估工具和公开基准，导致其迁移能力难以有效评估。
AI.method: 构建包含20个分类和35个检测数据集（均附加外部知识）的基准，开发自动超参调优工具包，并设计衡量样本效率和参数效率的多维度评估指标。
AI.result: ELEVATER作为CVinW平台发布，为语言增强视觉模型提供标准化评估框架和工具支持。
AI.conclusion: ELEVATER通过整合数据集、工具链和评估体系，解决了语言增强视觉模型迁移能力评估的系统性难题。

search_text: ELEVATER: A Benchmark and Toolkit for Evaluating Language-Augmented Visual Models ELEVATER：评估语言增强视觉模型的基准和工具包 Learning visual representations from natural language supervision has recently shown great promise in a number of pioneering works. In general, these language-augmented visual models demonstrate strong transferability to a variety of datasets and tasks. However, it remains challenging to evaluate the transferablity of these models due to the lack of easy-to-use evaluation toolkits and public benchmarks. To tackle this, we build ELEVATER (Evaluation of Language-augmented Visual Task-level Transfer), the first benchmark and toolkit for evaluating(pre-trained) language-augmented visual models. ELEVATER is composed of three components. (i) Datasets. As downstream evaluation suites, it consists of 20 image classification datasets and 35 object detection datasets, each of which is augmented with external knowledge. (ii) Toolkit. An automatic hyper-parameter tuning toolkit is developed to facilitate model evaluation on downstream tasks. (iii) Metrics. A variety of evaluation metrics are used to measure sample-efficiency (zero-shot and few-shot) and parameter-efficiency (linear probing and full model fine-tuning). ELEVATER is a platform for Computer Vision in the Wild (CVinW), and is publicly released at at https://computer-vision-in-the-wild.github.io/ELEVATER/ 从自然语言监督中学习视觉表征最近在许多开创性的工作中显示出了巨大的前景。一般来说，这些语言增强视觉模型表现出对各种数据集和任务的强大可迁移性。然而，由于缺乏易于使用的评估工具包和公共基准，评估这些模型的可移植性仍然具有挑战性。为了解决这个问题，我们构建了 ELEVATER（语言增强视觉任务级迁移的评估），这是第一个用于评估（预训练）语言增强视觉模型的基准和工具包。电梯由三部分组成。 (i) 数据集。作为下游评估套件，它由 20 个图像分类数据集和 35 个目标检测数据集组成，每个数据集都通过外部知识进行了增强。 (二) 工具包。开发了自动超参数调整工具包，以促进下游任务的模型评估。 (iii) 指标。使用各种评估指标来衡量样本效率（零样本和少样本）和参数效率（线性探测和全模型微调）。 ELEVATER 是一个野外计算机视觉 (CVinW) 平台，公开发布于 https://computer-vision-in-the-wild.github.io/ELEVATER/ 本文提出ELEVATER，一个用于评估语言增强视觉模型的基准和工具包，包含数据集、工具包和评估指标。 Chunyuan Li,Haotian Liu,Liunian Harold Li,Pengchuan Zhang,Jyoti Aneja,Jianwei Yang,Ping Jin,Houdong Hu,Zicheng Liu,Yong Jae Lee,Jianfeng Gao cs.CV,cs.CL,cs.LG

title: Where Was COVID-19 First Discovered? Designing a Question-Answering System for Pandemic Situations
title_zh: COVID-19 是在哪里首次发现的？设计针对流行病情况的问答系统
comment: Preprint accepted for archival and presentation at the 30th European Conference on Information Systems (ECIS 2022)
comments: Preprint accepted for archival and presentation at the 30th European Conference on Information Systems (ECIS 2022)
abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic is accompanied by a massive "infodemic" that makes it hard to identify concise and credible information for COVID-19-related questions, like incubation time, infection rates, or the effectiveness of vaccines. As a novel solution, our paper is concerned with designing a question-answering system based on modern technologies from natural language processing to overcome information overload and misinformation in pandemic situations. To carry out our research, we followed a design science research approach and applied Ingwersen's cognitive model of information retrieval interaction to inform our design process from a socio-technical lens. On this basis, we derived prescriptive design knowledge in terms of design requirements and design principles, which we translated into the construction of a prototypical instantiation. Our implementation is based on the comprehensive CORD-19 dataset, and we demonstrate our artifact's usefulness by evaluating its answer quality based on a sample of COVID-19 questions labeled by biomedical experts.
summary: The COVID-19 pandemic is accompanied by a massive "infodemic" that makes it hard to identify concise and credible information for COVID-19-related questions, like incubation time, infection rates, or the effectiveness of vaccines. As a novel solution, our paper is concerned with designing a question-answering system based on modern technologies from natural language processing to overcome information overload and misinformation in pandemic situations. To carry out our research, we followed a design science research approach and applied Ingwersen's cognitive model of information retrieval interaction to inform our design process from a socio-technical lens. On this basis, we derived prescriptive design knowledge in terms of design requirements and design principles, which we translated into the construction of a prototypical instantiation. Our implementation is based on the comprehensive CORD-19 dataset, and we demonstrate our artifact's usefulness by evaluating its answer quality based on a sample of COVID-19 questions labeled by biomedical experts.
abstract_zh: COVID-19 大流行伴随着大规模的“信息流行病”，这使得很难识别与 COVID-19 相关的问题的简明可靠的信息，例如潜伏时间、感染率或疫苗的有效性。作为一种新颖的解决方案，我们的论文涉及设计一个基于自然语言处理等现代技术的问答系统，以克服大流行情况下的信息过载和错误信息。为了开展我们的研究，我们遵循设计科学研究方法，并应用 Ingwersen 的信息检索交互认知模型，从社会技术角度为我们的设计过程提供信息。在此基础上，我们得出了设计要求和设计原则方面的规范性设计知识，并将其转化为原型实例的构建。我们的实施基于全面的 CORD-19 数据集，并且我们通过根据生物医学专家标记的 COVID-19 问题样本评估其答案质量来展示我们的工件的有用性。

AI.tldr: 本文设计了一个基于自然语言处理技术的问答系统，以应对疫情中的信息过载和虚假信息问题。
AI.motivation: 新冠疫情导致大量不准确信息传播，亟需有效工具帮助用户快速获取可信的疫情相关答案。
AI.method: 采用设计科学研究方法，结合Ingwersen的信息检索认知模型，基于CORD-19数据集构建原型系统，并通过专家标注问题评估系统效果。
AI.result: 成功开发出可有效回答疫情相关问题的问答系统原型，经专家评估验证了其答案质量。
AI.conclusion: 该系统为应对疫情等突发公共卫生事件的信息需求提供了可行的技术方案，具有推广价值。

title: Sensor Data Fusion in Top-View Grid Maps using Evidential Reasoning with Advanced Conflict Resolution
title_zh: 使用具有高级冲突解决功能的证据推理在俯视网格地图中融合传感器数据
abstract: We present a new method to combine evidential top-view grid maps estimated based on heterogeneous sensor sources. Dempster's combination rule that is usually applied in this context provides undesired results with highly conflicting inputs. Therefore, we use more advanced evidential reasoning techniques and improve the conflict resolution by modeling the reliability of the evidence sources. We propose a data-driven reliability estimation to optimize the fusion quality using the Kitti-360 dataset. We apply the proposed method to the fusion of LiDAR and stereo camera data and evaluate the results qualitatively and quantitatively. The results demonstrate that our proposed method robustly combines measurements from heterogeneous sensors and successfully resolves sensor conflicts.
summary: We present a new method to combine evidential top-view grid maps estimated based on heterogeneous sensor sources. Dempster's combination rule that is usually applied in this context provides undesired results with highly conflicting inputs. Therefore, we use more advanced evidential reasoning techniques and improve the conflict resolution by modeling the reliability of the evidence sources. We propose a data-driven reliability estimation to optimize the fusion quality using the Kitti-360 dataset. We apply the proposed method to the fusion of LiDAR and stereo camera data and evaluate the results qualitatively and quantitatively. The results demonstrate that our proposed method robustly combines measurements from heterogeneous sensors and successfully resolves sensor conflicts.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种新方法来结合基于异构传感器源估计的证据顶视图网格图。通常在这种情况下应用的登普斯特组合规则会通过高度冲突的输入提供不期望的结果。因此，我们使用更先进的证据推理技术，并通过对证据来源的可靠性进行建模来改进冲突解决。我们提出了一种数据驱动的可靠性估计，以使用 Kitti-360 数据集优化融合质量。我们将所提出的方法应用于激光雷达和立体相机数据的融合，并对结果进行定性和定量评估。结果表明，我们提出的方法稳健地结合了异构传感器的测量结果，并成功解决了传感器冲突。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于证据推理的异构传感器数据融合方法，用于生成顶视图网格地图。
AI.motivation: 传统Dempster组合规则在处理高冲突传感器数据时效果不佳，需改进冲突解决机制以提升融合质量。
AI.method: 采用数据驱动的证据可靠性估计模型，结合Kitti-360数据集优化融合效果，应用在LiDAR与立体相机数据融合中。
AI.result: 实验表明方法能有效融合异构传感器数据，定性和定量评估验证了冲突解决的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过建模证据源可靠性，所提方法显著提升了多传感器数据融合的鲁棒性与准确性。

We propose a data-driven reliability estimation to optimize the fusion quality using the Kitti-360 dataset. We apply the proposed method to the fusion of LiDAR and stereo camera data and evaluate the results qualitatively and quantitatively. The results demonstrate that our proposed method robustly combines measurements from heterogeneous sensors and successfully resolves sensor conflicts. 我们提出了一种新方法来结合基于异构传感器源估计的证据顶视图网格图。通常在这种情况下应用的登普斯特组合规则会通过高度冲突的输入提供不期望的结果。因此，我们使用更先进的证据推理技术，并通过对证据来源的可靠性进行建模来改进冲突解决。我们提出了一种数据驱动的可靠性估计，以使用 Kitti-360 数据集优化融合质量。我们将所提出的方法应用于激光雷达和立体相机数据的融合，并对结果进行定性和定量评估。结果表明，我们提出的方法稳健地结合了异构传感器的测量结果，并成功解决了传感器冲突。 本文提出一种基于证据推理的异构传感器数据融合方法，用于生成顶视图网格地图。 Sven Richter,Frank Bieder,Sascha Wirges,Christoph Stiller cs.CV

title: IndicXNLI: Evaluating Multilingual Inference for Indian Languages
title_zh: IndicXNLI：评估印度语言的多语言推理
abstract: While Indic NLP has made rapid advances recently in terms of the availability of corpora and pre-trained models, benchmark datasets on standard NLU tasks are limited. To this end, we introduce IndicXNLI, an NLI dataset for 11 Indic languages. It has been created by high-quality machine translation of the original English XNLI dataset and our analysis attests to the quality of IndicXNLI. By finetuning different pre-trained LMs on this IndicXNLI, we analyze various cross-lingual transfer techniques with respect to the impact of the choice of language models, languages, multi-linguality, mix-language input, etc. These experiments provide us with useful insights into the behaviour of pre-trained models for a diverse set of languages.
summary: While Indic NLP has made rapid advances recently in terms of the availability of corpora and pre-trained models, benchmark datasets on standard NLU tasks are limited. To this end, we introduce IndicXNLI, an NLI dataset for 11 Indic languages. It has been created by high-quality machine translation of the original English XNLI dataset and our analysis attests to the quality of IndicXNLI. By finetuning different pre-trained LMs on this IndicXNLI, we analyze various cross-lingual transfer techniques with respect to the impact of the choice of language models, languages, multi-linguality, mix-language input, etc. These experiments provide us with useful insights into the behaviour of pre-trained models for a diverse set of languages.
abstract_zh: 尽管印度 NLP 最近在语料库和预训练模型的可用性方面取得了快速进步，但标准 NLU 任务的基准数据集仍然有限。为此，我们引入了 IndicXNLI，这是 11 种印度语言的 NLI 数据集。它是通过原始英语 XNLI 数据集的高质量机器翻译创建的，我们的分析证明了 IndicXNLI 的质量。通过在此 IndicXNLI 上微调不同的预训练 LM，我们分析了各种跨语言迁移技术对语言模型选择、语言、多语言、混合语言输入等的影响。这些实验为我们提供了对不同语言的预训练模型行为的有用见解。

AI.tldr: 提出IndicXNLI数据集，评估多语言推理模型在印度语言中的表现。
AI.motivation: 现有印度语NLP缺乏标准NLU任务的基准数据集，需构建高质量多语言推理数据集以推动研究。
AI.method: 通过机器翻译构建IndicXNLI数据集，微调多种预训练模型，分析跨语言迁移效果及多语言因素影响。
AI.result: 验证了IndicXNLI数据集质量，揭示了预训练模型在跨语言迁移中的性能差异及多语言输入的影响。
AI.conclusion: IndicXNLI为印度语多语言推理研究提供了重要基准，揭示了模型与多语言因素间的复杂关系。

authors_text: Chenxin Xu,Maosen Li,Zhenyang Ni,Ya Zhang,Siheng Chen
title: GroupNet: Multiscale Hypergraph Neural Networks for Trajectory Prediction with Relational Reasoning
title_zh: GroupNet：利用关系推理进行轨迹预测的多尺度超图神经网络
abstract: Demystifying the interactions among multiple agents from their past trajectories is fundamental to precise and interpretable trajectory prediction. However, previous works only consider pair-wise interactions with limited relational reasoning. To promote more comprehensive interaction modeling for relational reasoning, we propose GroupNet, a multiscale hypergraph neural network, which is novel in terms of both interaction capturing and representation learning. From the aspect of interaction capturing, we propose a trainable multiscale hypergraph to capture both pair-wise and group-wise interactions at multiple group sizes. From the aspect of interaction representation learning, we propose a three-element format that can be learnt end-to-end and explicitly reason some relational factors including the interaction strength and category. We apply GroupNet into both CVAE-based prediction system and previous state-of-the-art prediction systems for predicting socially plausible trajectories with relational reasoning. To validate the ability of relational reasoning, we experiment with synthetic physics simulations to reflect the ability to capture group behaviors, reason interaction strength and interaction category. To validate the effectiveness of prediction, we conduct extensive experiments on three real-world trajectory prediction datasets, including NBA, SDD and ETH-UCY; and we show that with GroupNet, the CVAE-based prediction system outperforms state-of-the-art methods. We also show that adding GroupNet will further improve the performance of previous state-of-the-art prediction systems.
summary: Demystifying the interactions among multiple agents from their past trajectories is fundamental to precise and interpretable trajectory prediction. However, previous works only consider pair-wise interactions with limited relational reasoning. To promote more comprehensive interaction modeling for relational reasoning, we propose GroupNet, a multiscale hypergraph neural network, which is novel in terms of both interaction capturing and representation learning. From the aspect of interaction capturing, we propose a trainable multiscale hypergraph to capture both pair-wise and group-wise interactions at multiple group sizes. From the aspect of interaction representation learning, we propose a three-element format that can be learnt end-to-end and explicitly reason some relational factors including the interaction strength and category. We apply GroupNet into both CVAE-based prediction system and previous state-of-the-art prediction systems for predicting socially plausible trajectories with relational reasoning. To validate the ability of relational reasoning, we experiment with synthetic physics simulations to reflect the ability to capture group behaviors, reason interaction strength and interaction category. To validate the effectiveness of prediction, we conduct extensive experiments on three real-world trajectory prediction datasets, including NBA, SDD and ETH-UCY; and we show that with GroupNet, the CVAE-based prediction system outperforms state-of-the-art methods. We also show that adding GroupNet will further improve the performance of previous state-of-the-art prediction systems.
abstract_zh: 从过去的轨迹中揭开多个智能体之间的相互作用是精确且可解释的轨迹预测的基础。然而，以前的工作仅考虑具有有限关系推理的成对交互。为了促进更全面的关系推理交互建模，我们提出了 GroupNet，一种多尺度超图神经网络，它在交互捕获和表示学习方面都是新颖的。从交互捕获的角度来看，我们提出了一种可训练的多尺度超图来捕获多个组大小的成对和分组交互。从交互表示学习的角度来看，我们提出了一种可以端到端学习的三元素格式，并明确推理一些关系因素，包括交互强度和类别。我们将 GroupNet 应用于基于 CVAE 的预测系统和之前最先进的预测系统，以通过关系推理来预测社会上合理的轨迹。为了验证关系推理的能力，我们通过合成物理模拟进行实验，以反映捕获群体行为、推理交互强度和交互类别的能力。为了验证预测的有效性，我们对三个真实世界轨迹预测数据集（包括 NBA、SDD 和 ETH-UCY）进行了广泛的实验；我们证明，通过 GroupNet，基于 CVAE 的预测系统优于最先进的方法。我们还表明，添加 GroupNet 将进一步提高以前最先进的预测系统的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出GroupNet，一种多尺度超图神经网络，用于通过关系推理提升轨迹预测的准确性。
AI.motivation: 现有方法仅考虑成对交互，缺乏对群体行为和复杂关系的全面建模，难以实现精确且可解释的轨迹预测。
AI.method: 设计可训练的多尺度超图捕捉成对及群体交互，结合三元组表示学习，显式推理交互强度与类别，并集成到CVAE及先进预测系统中。
AI.result: 在合成物理模拟和NBA、SDD、ETH-UCY等真实数据集上验证，GroupNet显著提升预测效果，尤其使CVAE系统超越现有最优方法。
AI.conclusion: GroupNet通过多尺度群体交互建模与关系推理，有效提升了轨迹预测的准确性和可解释性。

title: Binary Multi Channel Morphological Neural Network
title_zh: 二元多通道形态神经网络
abstract: Neural networks and particularly Deep learning have been comparatively little studied from the theoretical point of view. Conversely, Mathematical Morphology is a discipline with solid theoretical foundations. We combine these domains to propose a new type of neural architecture that is theoretically more explainable. We introduce a Binary Morphological Neural Network (BiMoNN) built upon the convolutional neural network. We design it for learning morphological networks with binary inputs and outputs. We demonstrate an equivalence between BiMoNNs and morphological operators that we can use to binarize entire networks. These can learn classical morphological operators and show promising results on a medical imaging application.
summary: Neural networks and particularly Deep learning have been comparatively little studied from the theoretical point of view. Conversely, Mathematical Morphology is a discipline with solid theoretical foundations. We combine these domains to propose a new type of neural architecture that is theoretically more explainable. We introduce a Binary Morphological Neural Network (BiMoNN) built upon the convolutional neural network. We design it for learning morphological networks with binary inputs and outputs. We demonstrate an equivalence between BiMoNNs and morphological operators that we can use to binarize entire networks. These can learn classical morphological operators and show promising results on a medical imaging application.
abstract_zh: 从理论角度来看，神经网络，特别是深度学习的研究相对较少。相反，数学形态学是一门具有扎实理论基础的学科。我们结合这些领域提出了一种理论上更容易解释的新型神经架构。我们引入了基于卷积神经网络的二元形态神经网络（BiMoNN）。我们设计它是为了学习具有二进制输入和输出的形态网络。我们证明了 BiMoNN 和形态算子之间的等价性，我们可以用它来对整个网络进行二值化。它们可以学习经典的形态学算子，并在医学成像应用中显示出有希望的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于卷积神经网络的二值形态学神经网络（BiMoNN），用于学习具有二值输入和输出的形态学网络，并证明其与形态学算子的等价性。
AI.motivation: 深度学习理论研究不足，而数学形态学具有坚实的理论基础，因此结合两者以构建更可解释的神经网络架构。
AI.method: 设计基于卷积神经网络的二值形态学神经网络（BiMoNN），并通过证明其与形态学算子的等价性，实现网络的二值化。
AI.result: BiMoNN能够学习经典形态学算子，并在医学影像应用中表现出有前景的结果。
AI.conclusion: BiMoNN为深度学习提供了理论支撑，同时在实际应用中展现了有效性，为可解释性神经网络设计提供了新方向。

tldr: 本文提出一种基于卷积神经网络的二值形态学神经网络（BiMoNN），用于学习具有二值输入和输出的形态学网络，并证明其与形态学算子的等价性。
motivation: 深度学习理论研究不足，而数学形态学具有坚实的理论基础，因此结合两者以构建更可解释的神经网络架构。
method: 设计基于卷积神经网络的二值形态学神经网络（BiMoNN），并通过证明其与形态学算子的等价性，实现网络的二值化。
result: BiMoNN能够学习经典形态学算子，并在医学影像应用中表现出有前景的结果。
conclusion: BiMoNN为深度学习提供了理论支撑，同时在实际应用中展现了有效性，为可解释性神经网络设计提供了新方向。
search_text: Binary Multi Channel Morphological Neural Network 二元多通道形态神经网络 Neural networks and particularly Deep learning have been comparatively little studied from the theoretical point of view. Conversely, Mathematical Morphology is a discipline with solid theoretical foundations. We combine these domains to propose a new type of neural architecture that is theoretically more explainable. We introduce a Binary Morphological Neural Network (BiMoNN) built upon the convolutional neural network. We design it for learning morphological networks with binary inputs and outputs. We demonstrate an equivalence between BiMoNNs and morphological operators that we can use to binarize entire networks. These can learn classical morphological operators and show promising results on a medical imaging application. 从理论角度来看，神经网络，特别是深度学习的研究相对较少。相反，数学形态学是一门具有扎实理论基础的学科。我们结合这些领域提出了一种理论上更容易解释的新型神经架构。我们引入了基于卷积神经网络的二元形态神经网络（BiMoNN）。我们设计它是为了学习具有二进制输入和输出的形态网络。我们证明了 BiMoNN 和形态算子之间的等价性，我们可以用它来对整个网络进行二值化。它们可以学习经典的形态学算子，并在医学成像应用中显示出有希望的结果。 本文提出一种基于卷积神经网络的二值形态学神经网络（BiMoNN），用于学习具有二值输入和输出的形态学网络，并证明其与形态学算子的等价性。 Theodore Aouad,Hugues Talbot cs.CV,cs.LG

title: Modeling Missing Annotations for Incremental Learning in Object Detection
title_zh: 为对象检测中的增量学习建模缺失注释
abstract: Despite the recent advances in the field of object detection, common architectures are still ill-suited to incrementally detect new categories over time. They are vulnerable to catastrophic forgetting: they forget what has been already learned while updating their parameters in absence of the original training data. Previous works extended standard classification methods in the object detection task, mainly adopting the knowledge distillation framework. However, we argue that object detection introduces an additional problem, which has been overlooked. While objects belonging to new classes are learned thanks to their annotations, if no supervision is provided for other objects that may still be present in the input, the model learns to associate them to background regions. We propose to handle these missing annotations by revisiting the standard knowledge distillation framework. Our approach outperforms current state-of-the-art methods in every setting of the Pascal-VOC dataset. We further propose an extension to instance segmentation, outperforming the other baselines. Code can be found here: https://github.com/fcdl94/MMA
summary: Despite the recent advances in the field of object detection, common architectures are still ill-suited to incrementally detect new categories over time. They are vulnerable to catastrophic forgetting: they forget what has been already learned while updating their parameters in absence of the original training data. Previous works extended standard classification methods in the object detection task, mainly adopting the knowledge distillation framework. However, we argue that object detection introduces an additional problem, which has been overlooked. While objects belonging to new classes are learned thanks to their annotations, if no supervision is provided for other objects that may still be present in the input, the model learns to associate them to background regions. We propose to handle these missing annotations by revisiting the standard knowledge distillation framework. Our approach outperforms current state-of-the-art methods in every setting of the Pascal-VOC dataset. We further propose an extension to instance segmentation, outperforming the other baselines. Code can be found here: https://github.com/fcdl94/MMA
abstract_zh: 尽管物体检测领域最近取得了进展，但通用架构仍然不适合随着时间的推移逐步检测新类别。他们很容易遭受灾难性遗忘：他们在没有原始训练数据的情况下更新参数时忘记了已经学到的东西。先前的工作在目标检测任务中扩展了标准分类方法，主要采用知识蒸馏框架。然而，我们认为对象检测引入了一个被忽视的额外问题。虽然属于新类的对象是通过它们的注释来学习的，但如果没有为输入中可能仍然存在的其他对象提供监督，模型就会学习将它们与背景区域相关联。我们建议通过重新审视标准知识蒸馏框架来处理这些缺失的注释。我们的方法在 Pascal-VOC 数据集的每个设置中都优于当前最先进的方法。我们进一步提出了实例分割的扩展，其性能优于其他基线。代码可以在这里找到：https://github.com/fcdl94/MMA

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种针对目标检测任务中增量学习的缺失注释建模方法，以解决模型在学习新类别时对旧类别知识的遗忘问题。
AI.motivation: 现有增量学习方法在目标检测任务中忽视了未标注的旧类别对象会被误判为背景的问题，导致模型性能下降。
AI.method: 通过改进知识蒸馏框架，对缺失注释进行建模，利用教师模型的隐式监督引导学生模型学习新类别同时保留旧类别知识。
AI.result: 在Pascal-VOC数据集的所有设置中均优于现有最先进方法，在实例分割任务中也取得更好效果。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了目标检测增量学习中的缺失注释问题，为持续学习场景下的模型更新提供了新思路。

title: Speech Dereverberation with A Reverberation Time Shortening Target
title_zh: 具有混响时间缩短目标的语音去混响
abstract: This work proposes a new learning target based on reverberation time shortening (RTS) for speech dereverberation. The learning target for dereverberation is usually set as the direct-path speech or optionally with some early reflections. This type of target suddenly truncates the reverberation, and thus it may not be suitable for network training. The proposed RTS target suppresses reverberation and meanwhile maintains the exponential decaying property of reverberation, which will ease the network training, and thus reduce signal distortion caused by the prediction error. Moreover, this work experimentally study to adapt our previously proposed FullSubNet speech denoising network to speech dereverberation. Experiments show that RTS is a more suitable learning target than direct-path speech and early reflections, in terms of better suppressing reverberation and signal distortion. FullSubNet is able to achieve outstanding dereverberation performance.
summary: This work proposes a new learning target based on reverberation time shortening (RTS) for speech dereverberation. The learning target for dereverberation is usually set as the direct-path speech or optionally with some early reflections. This type of target suddenly truncates the reverberation, and thus it may not be suitable for network training. The proposed RTS target suppresses reverberation and meanwhile maintains the exponential decaying property of reverberation, which will ease the network training, and thus reduce signal distortion caused by the prediction error. Moreover, this work experimentally study to adapt our previously proposed FullSubNet speech denoising network to speech dereverberation. Experiments show that RTS is a more suitable learning target than direct-path speech and early reflections, in terms of better suppressing reverberation and signal distortion. FullSubNet is able to achieve outstanding dereverberation performance.
abstract_zh: 这项工作提出了一种基于混响时间缩短（RTS）的语音去混响新学习目标。去混响的学习目标通常设置为直接路径语音或可选地带有一些早期反射。此类目标会突然截断混响，因此可能不适合网络训练。所提出的RTS目标抑制混响，同时保持混响的指数衰减特性，这将简化网络训练，从而减少由预测误差引起的信号失真。此外，这项工作通过实验研究使我们之前提出的 FullSubNet 语音降噪网络适应语音去混响。实验表明，就更好地抑制混响和信号失真而言，RTS是比直接路径语音和早期反射更合适的学习目标。 FullSubNet能够实现出色的去混响性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于混响时间缩短（RTS）的新学习目标，用于改进语音去混响任务。
AI.motivation: 传统去混响方法使用直接路径语音或早期反射作为目标，会导致混响突然截断，影响网络训练并产生信号失真。
AI.method: 设计RTS目标以抑制混响同时保持指数衰减特性，并将FullSubNet去噪网络适配到去混响任务中。
AI.result: 实验表明RTS目标在抑制混响和减少失真方面优于传统方法，FullSubNet实现卓越去混响效果。
AI.conclusion: RTS目标比直接路径或早期反射更适合作为学习目标，FullSubNet框架有效提升去混响性能。
